_meta.purpose: Actionable task breakdown for all active goals. Add these tasks via the Life OS UI or seed script.
_meta.note: areaId and goalId values match your existing Convex data. Remove _id fields before inserting.

analysis.summary: You have 5 active yearly goals. Q1 ends in 9 days (April 1). Most goals are at 0% with strong constraints identified. The biggest pattern: you know what to do but struggle with execution. This breakdown focuses on DAILY ACTIONS over strategy.
analysis.biggestRisk: All 5 goals are interdependent and ambitious. Risk of doing nothing because everything feels too big. Solution: focus on ONE action per area per week.
queueOrder: Claude third (your clearest path to revenue)
Business fourth (emerges from Claude + Property overlap)
Property fifth (long game, execute 1 thing per week)
analysis.criticalInsight: Your business idea is already right in front of you: Claude automation for property developers. You are learning both. This is your niche.

goalBreakdowns.title: I am Musashi
goalBreakdowns.currentState: 28% body fat. Eats junk. Not tracking. Not going gym consistently.
goalBreakdowns.rootCause: No systems. Willpower alone fails. Need environment control + tracking.

quarterlyTargets.Q1 (ending April 1): Establish baseline: measurements taken, tracking app installed, junk food removed, gym schedule set.
quarterlyTargets.Q2 (ending July 1): Down to ~22% body fat. Gym 4x/week consistent. Meal prep weekly. Calories tracked 80% of days.
quarterlyTargets.Q3 (ending Oct 1): Down to ~18% body fat. Visible change. Habits are automatic.

weeklyNonNegotiables: Sunday meal prep: 14 portions (2 meals x 7 days)
Log every meal in tracking app
Weekly weight check every Monday morning
goalBreakdowns.dietPlan: Calculate TDEE (~2800-3200 cal). Eat at 500 cal deficit = 2300-2700 cal/day. Protein: 180g minimum. No junk food in house — zero tolerance on entry.
goalBreakdowns.gymProgram: PPL (Push/Pull/Legs): Push Mon, Pull Wed, Legs Fri, Upper Sat. Progressive overload: add 2.5kg when you hit top rep range for 2 sessions straight.

goalBreakdowns.title: Get Claude fully — become Claude's own engineer
goalBreakdowns.currentState: $0 revenue. Not in communities. Chasing multiple directions.
goalBreakdowns.targetState: $100k revenue from Claude/AI expertise by Dec 2026.
goalBreakdowns.rootCause: No niche. No audience. No portfolio. Chasing shiny objects instead of depth.

quarterlyTargets.Q1 (ending April 1): Pick 1 niche. Join 3 communities. Build 1 demo project.
quarterlyTargets.Q2 (ending July 1): First $1000. 2 paying clients. 10 LinkedIn posts with real engagement. 1 hackathon entered.
quarterlyTargets.Q3 (ending Oct 1): First $10k month. Speaking at 1 meetup. 100 LinkedIn followers in AI space.
quarterlyTargets.Q4 (ending Dec 31): $100k revenue. Melbourne's go-to Claude consultant for your niche.

goalBreakdowns.niche: Claude automation for property developers and real estate professionals (Melbourne focus). This connects directly to your Property Development goals.
goalBreakdowns.revenueModel: 1) Done-for-you Claude workflows: $2k-5k per client. 2) Training workshops: $500-1500 per session. 3) Consulting retainer: $3k-5k/month.
weeklyNonNegotiables: Post 1 piece of content on LinkedIn about Claude (what you built, what you learned)
Talk to 2 potential clients or industry contacts
Spend 5+ hours actually building with Claude Code
Join 1 new conversation in AI communities (Twitter/X, Discord)

goalBreakdowns.title: Run a full property deal end to end
goalBreakdowns.currentState: Learning but not executing. Scared to execute.
goalBreakdowns.targetState: Have completed analysis + planning of a full property development deal.
goalBreakdowns.rootCause: Analysis paralysis. Keeps learning without doing. Fear of making the wrong move.

quarterlyTargets.Q1 (ending April 1): Complete Module 1 + Assignment. Identify 1 mentor willing to trade Claude services.
quarterlyTargets.Q2 (ending July 1): Complete all 4 modules. Analyze 3 real deals (numbers only). Meet 1 developer doing live projects.
quarterlyTargets.Q3 (ending Oct 1): Shadow 1 live deal from DA to completion. Build a Claude tool for property feasibility.
quarterlyTargets.Q4 (ending Dec 31): Complete analysis of 1 real deal you could execute. Have borrowing capacity understood.

goalBreakdowns.executionFix: Every week you MUST produce 1 tangible output: a completed assignment, a deal analysis spreadsheet, a contact made, a site visited. Learning without output is procrastination.
goalBreakdowns.mentorOffer: Find property developers struggling with admin/spreadsheets. Offer 10 hours of Claude automation help in exchange for shadowing their process.

goalBreakdowns.title: 100k in bank account
goalBreakdowns.currentState: -$10,000. Unclear on what will actually create financial freedom.
goalBreakdowns.rootCause: No clear budget. No savings automation. Revenue unclear.

quarterlyTargets.Q1 (ending April 1): Know exact numbers: all income, all expenses, all debt. Budget set. Barefoot Investor read.
quarterlyTargets.Q2 (ending July 1): Break even to +$5k. Every dollar tracked. $500+/month automated savings.
quarterlyTargets.Q3 (ending Oct 1): +$25k. Revenue from Claude/Business starting to flow in.
quarterlyTargets.Q4 (ending Dec 31): +$100k through combination of saved income + business revenue.

goalBreakdowns.australianFIRE: Read The Barefoot Investor (Scott Pape) — the Australian money bible. Key system: Splurge/Smile/Fire accounts. ING high-interest savings. Automate everything.
immediateActions: List all debts with exact amounts and interest rates
Find and eliminate 3 subscriptions you don't use
Open ING Savings Maximiser (currently ~5% interest rate in AU)

goalBreakdowns.title: Start Business
goalBreakdowns.currentState: -$10,000. Keeps starting tarpit ideas. No validation.
goalBreakdowns.rootCause: Starts ideas without validating demand first. Builds before selling.

quarterlyTargets.Q1 (ending April 1): Talk to 20 people in target market. Identify the most painful problem. Do NOT build anything.
quarterlyTargets.Q2 (ending July 1): First $5k revenue. Pre-sell a service or solution to 2-3 customers before building.
quarterlyTargets.Q3 (ending Oct 1): First $50k revenue. Repeatable process. 1 case study published.
quarterlyTargets.Q4 (ending Dec 31): $200k+ revenue (be realistic — $500k is Year 1+, but aim for foundation).

goalBreakdowns.idea: Claude AI automation consultancy for property developers. Niche: help them with feasibility analysis, due diligence, council submission documents, and client reporting using Claude.
goalBreakdowns.validationRule: NEVER build before getting paid. First customer pays before you write 1 line of code. Charge $500 minimum for your first project even if it's manual work.
weeklyNonNegotiables: Talk to 5 people in target market (property developers, agents, project managers)
Write down the top 3 pain points you hear repeatedly
Send 10 cold outreach messages per week (LinkedIn/email)

tasks._note: Tasks are grouped by timeframe. Use areaId + goalId to link when creating in the app.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Take body measurements — weight, waist, hips + photos (baseline)
thisWeek_March23_30._why: You cannot track progress without a baseline. Do this today.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Download MyFitnessPal, log every meal for 7 days straight — start today
thisWeek_March23_30._why: Diet is 80% of body transformation. Tracking beats willpower every time.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Remove all junk food from the house — throw it out or give it away
thisWeek_March23_30._why: You can't out-willpower convenience. Control your environment.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Calculate your TDEE and set a daily calorie target (use TDEE calculator online)
thisWeek_March23_30._why: You need a number to hit. Target: TDEE minus 400-500 calories.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Set gym schedule for this week: Push Mon, Pull Wed, Legs Fri, Upper Sat — block it in calendar
thisWeek_March23_30._why: Decide when you go before the week starts. No decision = no gym.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: List ALL debts: credit card, loans, HECS — exact dollar amounts and interest rates
thisWeek_March23_30._why: You can't fix what you can't see. The -$10k needs a breakdown.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: List all monthly income (job, side income) and all monthly expenses — exact amounts
thisWeek_March23_30._why: Foundation of financial control. Write it down, not in your head.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Pick ONE niche for Claude consulting (decision: property developer automation in Melbourne)
thisWeek_March23_30._why: No niche = no clients. You can always change it. Just pick and commit for 90 days.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Join Anthropic Discord and 1 Claude/AI community — introduce yourself
thisWeek_March23_30._why: Network is how you get clients. Community is how you stay sharp.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Have 3 conversations with property developers or real estate people about their biggest workflow pain points
thisWeek_March23_30._why: VALIDATE before building. Their words = your product copy. Listen more than talk.

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Write down the #1 most painful manual task in property development that you heard this week
thisWeek_March23_30._why: Your niche = their pain point. One sentence: 'I help [who] do [what] faster using Claude.'

thisWeek_March23_30.title: Contact your property course provider and confirm which 4 modules to complete this quarter
thisWeek_March23_30._why: You need clarity on what Module 1 actually is before you can complete it.

q1_remaining_before_April1.title: Complete Module 1 of property course + submit assignment
q1_remaining_before_April1._why: Q1 milestone. If not done, Q2 starts behind.

q1_remaining_before_April1.title: Find 1 property developer on LinkedIn willing to chat — offer free Claude workflow demo
q1_remaining_before_April1._why: Trade value: your Claude skills for their industry knowledge. Win-win.

q1_remaining_before_April1.title: Cancel all unused subscriptions (audit bank statement right now)
q1_remaining_before_April1._why: Quick win. Every cancelled sub = recurring savings.

q1_remaining_before_April1.title: Order or download Barefoot Investor by Scott Pape — start reading
q1_remaining_before_April1._why: Best Australian money book. Read 1 chapter per day. Done in 2 weeks.

q1_remaining_before_April1.title: Post your first LinkedIn content about Claude — show something you built (even this app counts)
q1_remaining_before_April1._why: Your audience doesn't know you exist. First post is the hardest. Just ship it.

q1_remaining_before_April1.title: Sunday meal prep: cook 14 portions of protein + rice/potato + veggies for the week
q1_remaining_before_April1._why: No meal prep = junk food. Prep is the system that beats willpower.

q2_april_monthly.title: [WEEKLY HABIT] Gym Push session — chest, shoulders, triceps
q2_april_monthly._why: Non-negotiable. Log it every single week.

q2_april_monthly.title: [WEEKLY HABIT] Sunday meal prep — batch cook for the full week
q2_april_monthly._why: Without this, you will eat junk. This is the most important physical habit.

q2_april_monthly.title: Monthly body check-in: weight + body fat % + photos — adjust calories if needed
q2_april_monthly._why: If not losing 0.5-1kg/week, reduce calories by 200. If losing too fast, add 200.

q2_april_monthly.title: Complete Module 2 of property course + submit assignment
q2_april_monthly._why: 1 module per month = 4 modules done by Q2 end.

q2_april_monthly.title: Analyze 1 real property deal: find a listed development site, run the basic feasibility numbers
q2_april_monthly._why: This is execution. Not theory — look up an actual site on realcommercial.com.au and run the numbers.

q2_april_monthly.title: Finish reading Barefoot Investor + set up the bucket system (Splurge/Smile/Fire accounts)
q2_april_monthly._why: The book means nothing unless you set up the system. Set it up by end of April.

q2_april_monthly.title: Open ING Savings Maximiser (or UBANK) and transfer $500 minimum — automate future transfers
q2_april_monthly._why: Automated savings are the only savings that actually happen. Set it and forget it.

q2_april_monthly.title: Build 1 Claude demo specifically for property developers — feasibility calculator or document generator
q2_april_monthly._why: This is your portfolio piece AND your sales tool. Show it to 5 people for feedback.

q2_april_monthly.title: Get 1 paying client for Claude consulting — even $500 for a 2hr session counts
q2_april_monthly._why: First $1 changes everything psychologically. Charge something. Free clients waste your time.

q2_april_monthly.title: Talk to 20 property developers or agents this month — log pain points in a spreadsheet
q2_april_monthly._why: Validation. Look for the pain point 3+ people mention. That's your product.

q2_april_monthly.title: Write 1 case study or before/after story from a customer conversation or demo — post it publicly
q2_april_monthly._why: Social proof before you have clients. Document the process, share the insight.

q2_may_monthly.title: Monthly body check-in May: weight + body fat % + photos
Complete Module 3 of property course + submit assignment
Analyze 1 real property deal: contact a real estate agent and ask questions as if you're a developer
2 LinkedIn posts in May about Claude — specific workflow, specific result, specific industry
Reach $2,000 in Claude consulting revenue — track it
Net worth check: total assets minus total liabilities — is the number improving?
Decide on business offering: write 1 paragraph — who you serve, what you do, what it costs

q2_june_monthly.title: Monthly body check-in June: weight + body fat % + photos
Complete Module 4 of property course + submit final assignment — finish Q2 milestone
Meet face-to-face with 1 active property developer — shadow their process for half a day
Q2 Claude revenue review: have I hit $5k yet? If not, what's blocking it? Write down 3 specific changes.
Enter or submit for 1 Claude/AI hackathon or competition by end of Q2
Business: create a simple Linktree or landing page — name, what you do, how to contact you
Finance Q2 review: total saved so far, total debt remaining, net worth improvement

weeklyRhythm._description: This is what a winning week looks like across all goals. Print this and put it somewhere visible.
monday: Update goal progress in Life OS
tuesday: Deep work block: Claude coding / property course
thursday: Deep work block: business development calls / client work
friday: Weekly review: what got done, what didn't, why
saturday: Optional: property site visits or networking events
sunday: Plan next week's tasks in Life OS
Review finances: spending for the week

doNotDo: Do NOT start a new goal or side project until one of these is at 50%+ progress
Do NOT spend more than 2 hours 'researching' without producing a tangible output
Do NOT skip Sunday meal prep — this directly causes junk food eating all week
Do NOT build a product before talking to 10 potential customers
Do NOT miss a gym session without rescheduling it within the same week
Do NOT add more goals to this quarter — the 5 you have are enough
